question.text: The three ways of closing an option position are:

answer.text: Offset, exercise, and expiration (letting it expire worthless)
Exercise, margin call, and forced closure

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Three exit methods: (1) Offset — sell (buy) an identical option. (2) Exercise — on expiry for European options. (3) Expiration — let OTM option expire worthless. **The Trap:** Rollover, margin call, and forced closure are not standard option exit methods. The most common method is offset (squaring off). **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers the same three exit methods. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Most options are closed via offset before expiry — exercising is typically only done by hedgers who want physical delivery or futures positions.
